groundings.quote: Psychologist Carol Gilligan’s landmark work, In a Different Voice: Psychological Theory and Women’s Development (1982), disputes accounts of moral development that do not take into account girls’ moral experiences
The ethic of care has its roots in projects that aim to correct for the exclusion of women from traditional theorizations of moral reasoning.
Nel Noddings’s influential work, Caring: A Feminine Approach to Ethics and Moral Education (1984), argues for the moral preferability of a care perspective
